enrichment.short_description: Document management platform with a free Community Edition and paid Professional and Cloud editions for records, search, and workflow.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpenKM document management software: a free Community Edition plus paid Professional and Cloud editions for records, workflow, and search.

features.title: Document & records management
features.description: Centralized storage, retention, and organization of documents and records.

features.title: Workflow automation
features.description: Customizable automatic tasks and workflow tools to route documents through business processes.

features.title: Advanced search
features.description: Full-text and metadata-based search across stored documents.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Document-level security and permissions based on user roles.

features.title: Activity logging
features.description: Complete logging of user and document activity for auditability.

features.title: API & integration tools
features.description: Developer tools and APIs for extending and integrating OpenKM with other systems.

features.title: Modular architecture
features.description: Add-on modules extend the core platform's functionality.

pros: Free Community Edition available for evaluation and use
Three editions (Community, Professional, Cloud) scale from small teams to enterprises
Global network of regional offices across Europe, the Americas, Asia, and Africa
Localized deployments available for markets including Brazil, France, Germany, Italy, Mexico, Poland, Portugal, and Romania
cons: Starting with Community Edition 7.0, OpenKM no longer publishes the source code, distributing it only as a free closed binary
Professional and Cloud edition pricing is not published and requires contacting sales
Advanced workflow and governance features require upgrading beyond the free Community Edition

faqs.question: Does OpenKM offer a free version?
faqs.answer: Yes, OpenKM Community Edition is free to use, alongside paid Professional (self-hosted) and Cloud (subscription) editions.

faqs.question: Is OpenKM open source?
faqs.answer: Versions up to 6.3 were distributed under GPL-2.0 with published source code. Starting with Community Edition 7.0, OpenKM distributes only a free binary without publishing the source.

faqs.question: What editions does OpenKM offer?
faqs.answer: Community (free), Professional (paid, self-hosted), and Cloud (paid, subscription-based).

faqs.question: Where is OpenKM based?
faqs.answer: OpenKM is headquartered in Palma de Mallorca, Balearic Islands, Spain, with regional offices in Spain, the UK, the USA, and other markets.

use_cases.title: Records management
use_cases.description: Centralize document retention and records governance for regulated organizations.

use_cases.title: Workflow automation
use_cases.description: Automate document-driven approval and task workflows across departments.

features: Core document management, search, and workflow features

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OpenKM?
paragraphs: OpenKM is a document management platform that combines document and records management, workflow automation, and advanced search into one system. It is offered in three editions: a free Community Edition, a paid self-hosted Professional Edition, and a paid subscription Cloud Edition.
The company is headquartered in Palma de Mallorca, Spain, and maintains regional offices and localized versions of its site across the Americas, Europe, Asia, and Africa.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OpenKM is for
paragraphs: OpenKM suits organizations that need centralized document and records management with workflow automation, ranging from teams evaluating the free Community Edition to enterprises that require the Professional or Cloud editions and dedicated support.

enrichment.short_description: SeedDMS is a free, open-source, self-hosted document management system built on PHP with full-text search and LDAP support.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SeedDMS: a free, open-source PHP document management system with folder organization, full-text search, and LDAP authentication.

features.title: Hierarchical folder organization
features.description: Organizes documents in a folder structure similar to a traditional file system.

features.title: Full-text search
features.description: Indexes and searches document content across the repository.

features.title: Document preview & conversion
features.description: Generates previews and converts documents between formats.

features.title: LDAP authentication
features.description: Integrates with LDAP for enterprise directory-based authentication.

features.title: OAI-PMH support
features.description: Supports the OAI-PMH protocol for metadata harvesting.

features.title: WebDAV access
features.description: Provides WebDAV access to stored documents.

features.title: Notification system
features.description: Notifies users of workflow updates and document changes.

pros: Completely free and open source under GPLv2
Self-hosted, giving full control over data
LDAP integration for enterprise directory authentication
Actively developed for more than 10 years
WebDAV support for standard file-system-style access
cons: Requires self-hosting on a PHP/web server, with the associated setup and maintenance effort
No official commercial support tier found
Requires an environment that supports soft links (Unix-like systems)

faqs.question: Is SeedDMS free?
faqs.answer: Yes, SeedDMS is free and open-source software.

faqs.question: Is SeedDMS open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is licensed under the GNU General Public License version 2 (GPLv2).

faqs.question: Does SeedDMS support LDAP authentication?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports LDAP for enterprise directory integration.

faqs.question: Can SeedDMS preview documents?
faqs.answer: Yes, it generates document previews and supports format conversion.

use_cases.title: Document archiving
use_cases.description: Store, organize, and search scanned or digital documents in a self-hosted repository.

use_cases.title: Paperless workflows
use_cases.description: Use SeedDMS as a compatible server for paperless document capture setups.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SeedDMS?
paragraphs: SeedDMS is a free, open-source document management system built on PHP that organizes documents and digital media in a hierarchical folder structure. It supports full-text search, document preview and format conversion, LDAP authentication, WebDAV access, and OAI-PMH metadata harvesting.
The project has been developed for over a decade and is distributed under the GNU General Public License version 2 (GPLv2), with source code available for self-hosted deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SeedDMS is for
paragraphs: SeedDMS is suited to small and medium-sized organizations that want a free, self-hosted document management system and are comfortable running and maintaining a PHP-based web application.

enrichment.short_description: Alfresco, now part of Hyland, is an enterprise content, process, and governance management platform for document-centric business workflows.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Alfresco by Hyland: an enterprise content, process, and governance management platform for document-centric business workflows.

features.title: Web-based document management
features.description: Searchable access to content across devices through a web interface.

features.title: Integrated process management
features.description: Combines content and process services to speed up business decision-making.

features.title: Microsoft 365 & Google Docs integration
features.description: Connects with Microsoft 365 and Google Docs for collaborative editing.

features.title: Information governance
features.description: Automated lifecycle management with compliance capabilities.

features.title: Content intelligence
features.description: Automatic content enrichment and metadata extraction.

features.title: Open APIs
features.description: Built on open standards and APIs for customization and integration.

pros: Backed by Hyland's broader enterprise content services portfolio
Combines content management, process automation, and governance in one platform
Cited customers include Liberty Mutual Insurance, RBC Capital Markets, and Cisco
Both cloud and on-premises deployment options
Built on open standards and APIs for integration
cons: Pricing is not publicly disclosed and requires contacting sales
Enterprise-focused positioning may be costly or complex for very small teams
Now marketed under Hyland's broader portfolio, which may change roadmap or branding over time

faqs.question: Who owns Alfresco?
faqs.answer: Alfresco is now part of Hyland's content services portfolio.

faqs.question: What is Alfresco used for?
faqs.answer: It is used for enterprise document management, business process automation, and information governance.

faqs.question: Does Alfresco integrate with Microsoft 365?
faqs.answer: Yes, Alfresco integrates with Microsoft 365 and Google Docs for collaboration.

faqs.question: Is Alfresco pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, pricing is not published; organizations must contact Hyland's sales team for a quote.

use_cases.title: Regulated industry document management
use_cases.description: Centralize and govern content across healthcare, financial services, government, insurance, and other regulated sectors.

use_cases.title: Business process automation
use_cases.description: Combine content services with process automation to accelerate document-driven workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Alfresco?
paragraphs: Alfresco is a content, process, and governance management platform used to manage, process, and govern information across enterprises. It offers web-based document access, integrated business process management, information governance with automated lifecycle management, and content enrichment through metadata extraction.

Alfresco is now part of Hyland, positioned within Hyland's broader content services and intelligent document processing portfolio, and is deployed by organizations such as Liberty Mutual Insurance, RBC Capital Markets, and Cisco.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Alfresco is for
paragraphs: Alfresco is aimed at large organizations in industries such as healthcare, financial services, government, insurance, education, manufacturing, and retail that need enterprise-scale content management combined with process automation and compliance-driven governance.

enrichment.short_description: ownCloud is an open-source file sync, share, and collaboration platform with a free Community Edition and paid Standard/Enterprise tiers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ownCloud: open-source file sync and share software with a free Community Edition and paid Standard and Enterprise plans.

features.title: Spaces
features.description: Project folders for organizing and collaborating on team content.

features.title: Web Office integrations
features.description: In-browser document editing via OnlyOffice, Microsoft 365, or Collabora Online.

features.title: Multi-factor authentication & encryption
features.description: Security controls including MFA and data encryption.

features.title: Controlled public link sharing
features.description: Share files via links with password protection and expiration dates.

features.title: File lifecycle management
features.description: Manage files through their lifecycle within the platform.

features.title: Real-time document collaboration
features.description: Collaborate on documents in real time.

features.title: Desktop & mobile apps
features.description: Native apps for Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android.

pros: Free, open-source Community Edition with unlimited users
Broad platform coverage across desktop and mobile
Choice between self-hosting or ownCloud.online (SaaS hosted in Germany)
Notable users cited include CERN, ETH Zürich, the European Commission, and Fujitsu
Web Office integrations enable in-browser document editing
cons: Standard and Enterprise plans require a 25-user minimum and custom quote
Faster support SLAs (phone support, 2-hour reaction time) are limited to paid tiers
Advanced enterprise features and commercial licensing are not included in the free Community Edition

faqs.question: Is ownCloud free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition is free and open source with unlimited users; paid Standard and Enterprise tiers add support and enterprise features.

faqs.question: Does ownCloud offer a hosted option?
faqs.answer: Yes, ownCloud.online is a SaaS option hosted in Germany for individuals and teams.

faqs.question: What license does ownCloud use?
faqs.answer: Core components are licensed under Apache 2.0.

faqs.question: What is the minimum for Standard and Enterprise plans?
faqs.answer: Both require a minimum of 25 users, billed annually.

use_cases.title: Privacy-focused file sync
use_cases.description: Self-host file sync and share to keep data on-premises under organizational control.

use_cases.title: Team collaboration
use_cases.description: Use Spaces and Web Office to co-edit documents and organize team files.

features: 12x5 email and phone support, 2h reaction time

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ownCloud?
paragraphs: ownCloud is an open-source platform for secure file sharing, syncing, and real-time content collaboration. It offers a free Community Edition licensed under Apache 2.0, plus paid Standard and Enterprise editions with commercial licensing and support, and an ownCloud.online SaaS option hosted in Germany.
Features include Spaces for organizing team content, Web Office integrations with OnlyOffice, Microsoft 365, and Collabora Online, multi-factor authentication, and controlled public link sharing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ownCloud is for
paragraphs: ownCloud fits organizations that want self-hosted or Germany-hosted file sync and collaboration with control over data residency, ranging from individuals using the free Community Edition to enterprises on paid Standard or Enterprise plans.

enrichment.short_description: Seafile is an open-source file sync and share platform with a free Community Edition, paid Professional on-prem tier, and Plus cloud plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Seafile: open-source file sync and share software with a free Community Edition, a paid Professional tier, and Plus cloud plans from $8/user/month.

features.title: File sync with version control
features.description: Synchronizes files across devices while retaining version history.

features.title: AI-generated file properties
features.description: Assigns custom properties and AI-generated descriptions to files (Plus edition).

features.title: Multiple content views
features.description: View files as tables, galleries, kanban boards, or maps (Plus edition).

features.title: Real-time collaborative editing
features.description: Co-edit documents in real time via SeaDoc.

features.title: Team wiki
features.description: Built-in wiki functionality with version history.

features.title: Granular sharing permissions
features.description: Password-protected sharing with fine-grained permission controls.

features.title: Two-factor authentication & audit logs
features.description: Security controls available in the Professional edition.

pros: Free, open-source Community Edition
Professional edition pricing starts free for up to 3 users and $100/year total for 9 users
Reports over 1 million users, including universities and enterprises such as Kaspersky Lab
Both self-hosted (Professional) and managed cloud (Plus) deployment options
Plus cloud plan includes a free 30-day trial
cons: AI-powered organization and multi-view features are limited to the paid Plus cloud tier
Professional on-premises pricing scales per user, requiring a custom quote for 1,000+ users
Some collaboration features such as SeaDoc and kanban/map views are tied to specific paid editions

faqs.question: Is Seafile free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition is free and open source.

faqs.question: What does Seafile Professional cost?
faqs.answer: It is free for up to 3 users, $100/year total for 9 users, and then priced per user annually (e.g. $48/user/year for 10-249 users).

faqs.question: Is there a cloud-hosted option?
faqs.answer: Yes, Seafile Plus is a managed cloud plan starting at $8/user/month for the Team plan.

faqs.question: Does Seafile offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Plus plan includes a free 30-day trial for up to 3 users.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted file sync
use_cases.description: Run Seafile Community or Professional Edition on your own infrastructure for privacy and control.

use_cases.title: Team knowledge management
use_cases.description: Use Seafile Plus cloud for wikis, collaborative editing, and AI-assisted file organization.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Seafile?
paragraphs: Seafile is an open-source file synchronization and sharing platform. Its free Community Edition covers core sync, versioning, and public link sharing, while the paid Professional edition (self-hosted) adds office file editing, full-text search, two-factor authentication, and audit logs, and the Plus cloud plan adds knowledge management, custom file properties, multiple content views, and AI-assisted metadata extraction.
Seafile reports over 1 million users across enterprises, universities, and other organizations, including customers such as Kaspersky Lab.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Seafile is for
paragraphs: Seafile suits organizations that want self-hosted control via the free Community or paid Professional edition, as well as teams that prefer a managed cloud option through Seafile Plus for wiki, collaboration, and AI-assisted file organization features.

enrichment.short_description: Pydio Cells is a self-hosted content collaboration platform with an open-source AGPLv3 Community edition and paid enterprise licensing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pydio Cells: self-hosted content collaboration platform with a free, open-source AGPLv3 Community edition and paid enterprise licensing.

features.title: Self-hosted file transfers
features.description: Handles file transfers up to 5TB on infrastructure you control.

features.title: No-code workflow automation
features.description: Cells Flows lets users build automated workflows without code.

features.title: SSO & two-factor authentication
features.description: Advanced security including single sign-on and 2FA.

features.title: Granular access controls
features.description: Access management via ACLs and the platform's "Cells" concept.

features.title: Metadata management
features.description: Automated tagging and metadata management for content.

features.title: Hybrid/private cloud compatibility
features.description: Deployable in hybrid or private cloud environments.

features.title: API access
features.description: Documented API for developer integrations.

pros: Free, open-source Community/Home edition licensed under AGPLv3
Full self-hosted control over data
Used by large organizations including Crédit Agricole, Seagate, and the University of Cambridge, per the vendor
No-code workflow builder (Cells Flows) included
API documentation available for custom integrations
cons: Enterprise pricing is not published and requires contacting sales
Self-hosting requires server infrastructure and setup expertise
Premium support is limited to the paid enterprise tier

faqs.question: Is Pydio Cells open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is licensed under AGPLv3.

faqs.question: Is there a free edition?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pydio Cells Home is a free, open-source edition for individual use.

faqs.question: How much does Pydio Cells Enterprise cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing is not published; organizations need to contact sales for a quote.

faqs.question: What is the maximum file transfer size?
faqs.answer: Pydio Cells supports file transfers up to 5TB.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted document sharing
use_cases.description: Manage and share large files and digital assets on infrastructure you control.

use_cases.title: Secure collaboration in regulated industries
use_cases.description: Use granular ACLs, SSO, and 2FA for secure content collaboration.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pydio Cells?
paragraphs: Pydio Cells is a self-hosted content collaboration platform for document sharing, digital asset management, and secure collaboration. It supports file transfers up to 5TB, no-code workflow automation via Cells Flows, SSO and two-factor authentication, and granular access controls.
The Community/Home edition is free and open source under AGPLv3, while the Enterprise edition adds premium support and is priced on request.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pydio Cells is for
paragraphs: Pydio Cells suits organizations that need self-hosted document collaboration with full data control, from individuals using the free Home edition to enterprises such as those in finance, healthcare, and government evaluating the paid Enterprise tier.

enrichment.short_description: FileRun is a self-hosted, proprietary file sync and share platform with one-time and per-user annual pricing, and no ongoing free tier.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: FileRun: self-hosted file sync and share software with one-time Family licensing and per-user annual Business plans, no free tier.

features.title: Self-hosted file sync & share
features.description: Sync, share, and collaborate on files on your own server.

features.title: Multiple authentication methods
features.description: Supports LDAP, Active Directory, OAuth2, and WordPress authentication.

features.title: Custom branding
features.description: Branding and theming customization options.

features.title: Online document editing
features.description: Integrates with ONLYOFFICE, Collabora, Google Docs, and Microsoft 365.

features.title: Advanced uploads
features.description: Folder sync, resumable transfers, and drag-and-drop uploads.

features.title: Desktop & mobile apps
features.description: Apps for Mac, Windows, Linux, iOS, and Android.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Runs on Linux/Windows servers, NAS systems, Docker, VMs, bare metal, or Raspberry Pi.

pros: Full self-hosted control, no reliance on third-party cloud storage
Broad platform support with desktop and mobile apps
Multiple authentication integrations (LDAP, AD, OAuth2, WordPress)
Runs on low-cost hardware such as Raspberry Pi
One-time Family license option for personal, non-commercial use
cons: No ongoing free tier for regular use
Family license is restricted to non-commercial use only
Business plans require an annual per-user subscription
Proprietary software, not open source

faqs.question: Is FileRun free?
faqs.answer: No, FileRun requires a paid license: a one-time Family plan or an annual per-user Business plan. A free trial is available for large business customers on request.

faqs.question: Is FileRun open source?
faqs.answer: No, FileRun is proprietary commercial software.

faqs.question: What authentication methods does FileRun support?
faqs.answer: LDAP, Active Directory, OAuth2, and WordPress.

faqs.question: Can FileRun run on a Raspberry Pi?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports budget hardware including Raspberry Pi.

use_cases.title: Personal cloud storage
use_cases.description: Self-host a Dropbox/Google Drive alternative under the one-time Family license.

use_cases.title: Business file sharing
use_cases.description: Sync, share, and co-edit documents on company-controlled servers with LDAP/AD integration.

features: 9 free guest accounts per paid user

long_form_blocks.heading: What is FileRun?
paragraphs: FileRun is a self-hosted file synchronization and sharing platform that lets users sync, share, and collaborate on files on their own server instead of a third-party cloud service. It supports LDAP, Active Directory, OAuth2, and WordPress authentication, online document editing via ONLYOFFICE, Collabora, Google Docs, or Microsoft 365, and desktop and mobile apps for Mac, Windows, Linux, iOS, and Android.
FileRun is proprietary software, sold as a one-time Family license for non-commercial use or as an annual per-user subscription for Small, Medium, and Large Business plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who FileRun is for
paragraphs: FileRun fits individuals or families who want a low-cost, one-time-licensed self-hosted cloud alternative, as well as businesses that need self-hosted file sharing with LDAP/AD integration and per-user annual licensing.

name: Cozy Cloud
skip_reason: Cozy Cloud was acquired by Linagora and, starting July 2025, has been rebranded and merged into Twake Workplace (Cozy Drive/Pass/Notes became Twake Drive/Pass/Notes). The official site (cozy.io) now serves Twake Workplace content, not a product operating under the 'Cozy Cloud' name, so no editorial content can be verified for the listed product name.

enrichment.short_description: Peergos is an open-source, end-to-end encrypted cloud storage platform you can self-host free or use via paid hosted plans from £3/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Peergos: open-source, end-to-end encrypted cloud storage. Self-host for free or use hosted plans starting at £3/month.

features.title: Post-quantum encryption
features.description: Protects files in transit and at rest with post-quantum encryption.

features.title: Protected metadata
features.description: Hides contact lists, file sizes, and directory structures, even from Peergos itself.

features.title: Offline access
features.description: Log in and view recently accessed files without an internet connection.

features.title: Secure sync client
features.description: Synchronizes files across multiple devices.

features.title: Portable identity
features.description: Migrate between servers while retaining data and connections.

features.title: Secret shareable links
features.description: Share files with people who don't have a Peergos account.

features.title: Built-in apps
features.description: Includes a document editor, spreadsheets, calendar, media player, and PDF viewer.

pros: Fully open source (AGPL-3.0) with code published on GitHub
Free to self-host indefinitely
Independently security-audited by Cure53 and Radically Open Security
Post-quantum encryption and hidden metadata for strong privacy
Backed by grant/foundation funding (NGI-POINTER, Protocol Labs, FUTO, Oxford University) rather than venture capital
cons: No free hosted (server-based) plan; only self-hosting is free
Hosted plan storage caps (200GB-3000GB) are modest versus mainstream cloud providers
Android is supported but no iOS app was found

faqs.question: Is Peergos free?
faqs.answer: You can self-host Peergos for free. Hosted plans start at £3/month (billed yearly).

faqs.question: Is Peergos open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is licensed under AGPL-3.0 with code available on GitHub.

faqs.question: What encryption does Peergos use?
faqs.answer: Post-quantum end-to-end encryption for files in transit and at rest.

faqs.question: Has Peergos been security audited?
faqs.answer: Yes, by Cure53 and Radically Open Security, with reports published publicly.

use_cases.title: Encrypted personal cloud storage
use_cases.description: Store and share photos, videos, music, and documents with end-to-end encryption.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted encrypted storage
use_cases.description: Run your own Peergos server for free while still connecting to users on other hosts.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Peergos?
paragraphs: Peergos is an open-source, end-to-end encrypted cloud storage and file-sharing platform that uses post-quantum encryption to protect files in transit and at rest, and hides metadata such as contact lists, file sizes, and directory structures. It is available for Windows, macOS, Linux, and Android.

The project is open source under AGPL-3.0, with code published on GitHub, and has undergone independent security audits by Cure53 and Radically Open Security. Users can self-host Peergos for free or subscribe to hosted plans starting at £3/month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Peergos is for
paragraphs: Peergos is aimed at privacy-focused individuals who want end-to-end encrypted cloud storage, and at technical users who prefer to self-host their own encrypted storage server for free while still being able to connect with users on other Peergos servers.

enrichment.short_description: Filestash is an open-source, self-hosted file manager for 30+ storage backends, with a free Hobby tier and paid Self Served/Enterprise plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Filestash: open-source file manager for S3, SFTP, WebDAV, and 30+ storage backends. Free self-hosted Hobby tier, paid plans from $50/month.

features.title: Multi-protocol storage support
features.description: Unified interface for S3, SFTP, FTP, WebDAV, SMB, NFS, Git, SharePoint, and 30+ other backends.

features.title: SSO integration
features.description: Authenticate via LDAP, SAML, and OIDC.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Granular authorization, including AWS IAM support.

features.title: Gateway functionality
features.description: Expose data via SFTP, S3, or MCP protocols.

features.title: White-label branding
features.description: Customizable branding for resale, available on paid plans.

features.title: Workflow automation
features.description: Event-triggered task orchestration.

features.title: Audit & compliance logging
features.description: Immutable, tamper-proof logs supporting GDPR compliance.

features.title: AI features
features.description: Semantic search, smart folders, and OCR, with Ollama support.

pros: Open-source (AGPLv3) Hobby tier, free to self-host for up to 3 users
Very broad protocol and storage backend support (30+)
Cited enterprise/institutional clients include MIT, JP Morgan, Bell, Orange, and UCI
AI features such as semantic search and OCR available at higher tiers
Both self-hosted and cloud deployment options
cons: Free Hobby tier is capped at 3 users
Enterprise features (high availability, whitelabeling, 24/7 support) start at $290/month
SSO and RBAC require the paid Self Served commercial license, not just the free tier

faqs.question: Is Filestash free?
faqs.answer: The Hobby tier is free for self-hosted use with up to 3 users, or $6/month for a hosted Hobby plan.

faqs.question: Is Filestash open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Hobby tier is licensed under AGPLv3 and available on GitHub.

faqs.question: What storage backends does Filestash support?
faqs.answer: S3, SFTP, FTP, WebDAV, SMB, NFS, Git, SharePoint, and 30+ other storage protocols.

faqs.question: Does Filestash offer AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, semantic search, smart folders, and OCR, with support for Ollama.

use_cases.title: Free self-hosted file browser
use_cases.description: Run a unified file manager across storage backends at no cost for small teams.
use_cases.audience: Individuals and small teams (up to 3 users)

use_cases.title: Enterprise storage consolidation
use_cases.description: Provide SSO, RBAC, audit logging, and compliance controls across multiple storage systems.
use_cases.audience: Enterprises in finance, technology, and government

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Filestash?
paragraphs: Filestash is a self-hosted file management platform that provides a unified interface for browsing, organizing, and sharing files across 30+ storage backends, including S3, SFTP, FTP, WebDAV, SMB, NFS, Git, and SharePoint. It supports SSO via LDAP, SAML, and OIDC, role-based access control with AWS IAM support, and AI features such as semantic search, smart folders, and OCR with Ollama support.

The free, open-source Hobby tier (AGPLv3) supports self-hosting for up to 3 users, while paid Self Served and Enterprise plans add commercial licensing, SSO/RBAC, white-labeling, audit and compliance logging, and 24/7 support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Filestash is for
paragraphs: Filestash suits individuals or small teams wanting a free, self-hosted unified file browser across cloud storage backends, as well as enterprises that need SSO, RBAC, audit logging, and compliance controls across multiple storage systems.
